title: perf(downloads): custom-node install slow — swap pure-JS extract-zip for native tar
description:
During a 1.1.0 install the user saw custom-node extraction run "forever". Investigated + fixed.

DIAGNOSIS (measured on Windows, comfyui_controlnet_aux source tree = 747 files / ~49MB, 3-run avg):
  extract-zip (pure-JS yauzl, the old code): 1299 ms
  native tar -xf on the SAME .zip:             476 ms  (2.7x faster)
  native tar -xf on a .tar (no compression):   318 ms  (4x, for reference only)
The bottleneck is NOT compression and NOT bytes — it is FILE COUNT. extract-zip's single-file-at-a-time JS write loop is the villain. (The 1.3GB inside controlnet_aux is a runtime-downloaded depth-anything weight under ckpts/, NOT in the node archive — the archive is small source, just many files.)

FIX (routes/downloadManager.js `_extractZipArchive`, ~L46): try native `tar -xf` first (via child_process execFile), fall back to extract-zip on ANY failure. bsdtar/libarchive (Windows tar.exe 3.8.4 + macOS tar) reads zip natively; the download URLs / node_lock.json / .mpi_node_commit markers / downstream folder-rename scan are all UNTOUCHED. Verified bsdtar output is byte-identical layout to extract-zip (`diff -rq` clean) so the `<Repo>-<branch>/` scan-and-rename step behaves the same.

WHY THIS IS SAFE ON THE TWO AXES THE USER FLAGGED (the register-in-case-it-bites-us part):
  1. RAM — the swap REDUCES OOM risk, does not add it. bsdtar streams one entry at a time in a SEPARATE process (constant memory, independent of file count / archive size); extract-zip holds the whole central-directory entry list in Electron's JS heap. No unzip-OOM event exists in this repo's git history (searched -S extract-zip / adm-zip / unzipper / yauzl — extract-zip has been the ONLY extractor since MPI-8 portable foundation; whatever OOM the user remembered was a different project or path). If a future extraction OOMs, it is NOT this change — look upstream.
  2. Mac / Linux / Pod:
     - Pod/remote: NEVER runs this path. `_startRemoteDownload` sets modelJob.installCustomNodes=false (downloadManager.js ~L1224) — nodes are image-resident/baked on the Pod. Untouched.
     - macOS: tar = bsdtar/libarchive -> reads zip -> fast path.
     - Linux: GNU tar canNOT read zip -> the try/catch FALLS BACK to extract-zip -> ZERO regression, just no speedup there. THIS IS THE ONE KNOWN TRAP: if someone "optimizes" the fallback away, Linux custom-node installs break. Keep the fallback.

BITE-BACK WATCHLIST (if this regresses):
  - Linux install fails to extract a node -> the fallback was removed or tar started matching+failing without throwing. Restore extract-zip fallback.
  - A node lands with wrong top-level folder name -> an extractor produced a different top-level layout than extract-zip; re-check the byte-identical-layout assumption for that specific archive (Comfy Registry node.zip vs GitHub /archive/ may differ).
  - Some minimal container ships no `tar` at all -> execFile throws ENOENT -> fallback covers it (logged as warn 'native tar extract failed ... falling back').

OPTIONAL FUTURE (NOT done, deliberately): re-host nodes as uncompressed .tar on R2 to shave a further ~160ms and drop decompression. Rejected as not worth it — touches lockUrl() + R2 uploads + Pod image for 160ms. Ponytail: don't.

NOT COMMITTED at time of carding (shared tree). One-file change.
